Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Continued To Oct-12-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to compel discovery responses is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. Plaintiff Bailey Lim is ordered to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses to defendant Jonathan Murphy's first set of special interrogatories; defendant Julia Fox's first set of special interrogatories; defendant Steven Betz's first set of special interrogatories; defendants' first set of form interrogatories; defendant Rains Lucia Stern St. Phalle and Silver's (Rains Lucia) first set of special interrogatories; and defendants Rains Lucia's first set of requests for production of documents. Responses shall be provided within 10 days. All responsive documents shall be produced within 25 days. Defendants do not seek sanctions. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order that recites the court's ruling verbatim. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JONATHAN MURPHY, JULIA FOX, STEVEN BETZ, RAINS LUCIA STERN ST. PHALLE, and SILVER, PC's Motion For Order Establishing Admissions.

Appearances:
Stanley Goff, Esq., Law Offices of, via Zoom videoconference, (415) 571-9570, for Plaintiff.

Douglas J. Melton, Esq., of Long & Levit, LLP, in person, (415) 397-2222, for Defendants.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion for order establishing admissions is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Because plaintiff Bailey Lim has not served responses to defendants' first set of requests for admission, all of the requests in that set are deemed admitted.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)
